Cache Firebase Storage Images with Cloudflare Workers — Completely Free Bandwidth Savings

If you're using Firebase Storage to host blog images like I was, you've probably noticed bandwidth costs creeping up — every article view pulls the images straight from Firebase Storage, and all of it lands on your Firebase bill. This post walks through how to use Cloudflare Workers plus a custom subdomain to cache those images. The whole setup is free and doesn't require a credit card.
macOS Time Machine Taking Forever? One Trick to Speed It Up

macOS Time Machine is a great backup tool, but the backup process sometimes gets painfully slow — especially if you haven't run it for a while. This post walks through the fastest way to get Time Machine going again, so backing up stops being a chore.
Chrome Keeps Caching Your Changes? Three Ways to Clear It Fast

You updated the page but Chrome keeps showing the old version. This post walks through three ways to clear Chrome's cache — keyboard shortcut, devtools, and an extension — plus a tip for how developers can avoid the problem in the first place.
What Is a Port? Why Does Everyone Use 3000? A Practical Guide to Picking Ports

A port is how your machine tells network services apart — and 3000 is just a safe, unreserved default that dev tools standardised on. This post covers what ports are, how the reserved ranges work, how to pick one for your project, and how to find out what's hogging a port.
Google Domains Shut Down — Buying a Domain on Cloudflare and Deploying a Site, Step by Step

I wrote a domain-buying tutorial way back, using Google Domain as the example. But Google Domain announced shutdown on September 7, 2023, so here's the Cloudflare Domain version — registering a domain, deploying a static site with Pages, and migrating an existing Squarespace (post-Google-Domain) domain over.
No Postman? No Problem — Testing APIs with curl
Sometimes you can't use Postman to test an API and you can't install other tools — maybe you're SSH'd into a server (servers usually have no GUI). That's when you reach for curl.
The Browser Render Pipeline Every Frontend Engineer Should Know

A student asked me about this recently, and I think it's actually important stuff for frontend engineers. We're constantly working with browsers — not knowing how they work seems like a missed opportunity.
Raycast Extensions I Actually Use — Level Up Your Raycast

A friend asked why my project switching and dev workflow feels so snappy on Raycast — turns out it's mostly the extensions. This post is a rundown of the Raycast extensions I actually keep installed: TinyPNG, Kill Process, Visual Studio Code, Google Translate, Speedtest, Format JSON, Base64, and JWT Decoder — with my aliases for each.
Zero-Width Space (ZWSP): The Invisible Character That Breaks Your Code

Ever had a string that looks like plain "ABC" or "123" on screen but won't run as code or won't post as a URL? That's often a "ghost character" — specifically the zero-width space. This post covers what zero-width space (ZWSP) is, why it sneaks into your code, and how to find and remove it before it eats your afternoon.
Team Communication: Resolving Conflict, Building Consensus and Trust

Anywhere people work together, communication friction is inevitable. This post is my experience on how to defuse it — understanding the other side's perspective, when to bring in a neutral mediator, building consensus and trust through 1:1 conversation, picking the right way to deliver solutions (group announcement vs. DM vs. meeting), and giving people time to adjust afterwards.
